title: inula subfloccosa
intro: Inula subfloccosa, also known as the woolly-headed elecampane or woolly inula, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family, native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of inula subfloccosa include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Inula subfloccosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
